full_prompt: Design a forged aluminum front suspension steering knuckle for a mid-size passenger vehicle (sprung mass ~1,800 kg) per a canonical automotive-suspension-knuckle patent concept (US20180257713A1-style): carry wheel vertical + lateral + longitudinal loads, support the lower ball joint, upper strut, tie rod, brake caliper, and wheel hub bearing. Envelope: 300 x 200 x 150 mm. Interfaces: wheel-hub bearing bore 85 mm H7 with 4-bolt pattern M12 on 90 mm PCD; lower ball joint taper bore Morse #6 (18 mm small dia); strut-mounting two-bolt clevis for M14 grade 10.9 bolts 50 mm apart; tie rod tapered hole 16 mm small dia; brake caliper mounting two-bolt pattern M12 x 1.5 on 90 mm center (ISO/TS 16949 caliper interface). Material: forged aluminum AA6082-T6 (yield 260 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, E 70 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3, elongation 10%). Representative load cases derived from SAE Fatigue Design Handbook ground-vehicle wheel-load spectra for a 1,800 kg sedan with 450 kg per front wheel static: LC1 vertical 3 g = 450 x 3 x 9.81 = 13,240 N at wheel center; LC2 lateral 0.9 g = 3,970 N (cornering / g-out); LC3 longitudinal 1.0 g = 4,414 N (brake locked-wheel); LC4 combined vertical + lateral at 0.8 each = 10,592 + 3,176 N; LC5 pot-hole impact 5 g vertical + 1 g lateral = 22,068 + 4,414 N; LC6 fatigue spectrum at SAE wheel-load time history for 300,000 km vehicle life with steel-castable damage-equivalent stress. Pass/fail per SAE J1099 + automotive chassis practice: (R1) max von Mises static (LC1-4) <= yield / 1.5 = 173 MPa; (R2) max von Mises pot-hole (LC5) <= UTS = 310 MPa (shall not fracture at pot-hole); (R3) fatigue life under LC6 spectrum >= 300,000 km using AA6082-T6 S-N (fatigue limit ~95 MPa at 2e6 cycles); (R4) steering toe-in precision: wheel-carrier rotation under LC2 lateral <= 0.1 deg (handling precision); (R5) mass <= 3.5 kg (typical forged-alu knuckle mass for this class); (R6) ball-joint taper pull-out force >= 60 kN (ISO 25921 or SAE J491 equivalent); (R7) caliper mounting interface max deflection under brake torque LC3 <= 0.1 mm (to avoid brake pad taper wear). Deliverables: STEP AP242 forging neutral body (pre-machining) + finish-machined body; FEA static + fatigue; ball-joint pull-out check; mass statement.
short_prompt:
Design a forged aluminum front suspension steering knuckle for a mid-size passenger vehicle (sprung mass ~1,800 kg) per a canonical automotive-suspension-knuckle patent concept (US20180257713A1-style): carry wheel vertical + lateral + longitudinal loads; support the lower ball joint, upper strut, tie rod, brake caliper, and wheel hub bearing.

Envelope: 300 x 200 x 150 mm. Interfaces: wheel-hub bearing bore 85 mm H7 with four M12 on 90 mm PCD; lower ball-joint taper bore Morse #6 (18 mm small dia); strut-mount two-bolt clevis for M14 class 10.9 bolts 50 mm apart; tie-rod tapered hole 16 mm small dia; brake caliper two-bolt pattern M12 x 1.5 on 90 mm (ISO/TS 16949).

Material: forged AA6082-T6.

Load cases derived from SAE Fatigue Design Handbook for a 1,800 kg sedan (450 kg per front wheel static).
- LC1 vertical 3 g: 13,240 N at wheel center.
- LC2 lateral 0.9 g: 3,970 N (cornering/g-out).
- LC3 longitudinal 1.0 g: 4,414 N (brake locked-wheel).
- LC4 combined vertical + lateral at 0.8 each: 10,592 + 3,176 N.
- LC5 pot-hole impact 5 g vertical + 1 g lateral: 22,068 + 4,414 N.
- LC6 SAE wheel-load spectrum for 300,000 km life.

Gates include steering-toe wheel-carrier rotation under LC2 <= 0.1 deg; mass <= 3.5 kg; ball-joint taper pull-out >= 60 kN (ISO 25921 or SAE J491); caliper-interface deflection under LC3 <= 0.1 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Rainflow + Miner's rule for 300,000 km life spectrum.
Taper interference pull-out is a physical-test metric.

prompt.title: Forged aluminum front suspension knuckle, 1,800 kg sedan (automotive-suspension-knuckle patent US20180257713A1 style)
prompt.brief: Design a 300x200x150 mm AA6082-T6 front knuckle surviving 3 g vertical + 0.9 g lateral + pot-hole 5 g; 300,000 km fatigue life; mass <=3.5 kg.

boundary_conditions.hub_bearing: wheel load introduced through bearing bore

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: AA6082-T6 fatigue limit 95 MPa at 2e6 cycles + SAE spectrum

requirements.governing_standard: SAE J1099 + SAE J491 + FMVSS 135 (brake) + NHTSA chassis durability guidance + ISO 25921

analysis_types: linear static x5 + fatigue spectrum post-proc

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Brake thermal loading is a secondary concern, out of scope here.

notes.patent_fetch: US20180257713A1 specific load claims need verification. Magnitudes are derived from 1,800 kg sedan class.
notes.numeric_source: SAE J1099 fatigue design; AA6082-T6 allowables; typical mid-size sedan wheel loads.
